af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. imgix image optimization and CDN. API key + URL signature for secure image transformations. Real-time transformations via URL parameters (similar to ImageKit). Source management and purge API. Good multi-language SDK. Transformations are cached on CDN — fast repeated access. Rate limits by plan. Excellent for media pipeline agents requiring dynamic image optimization and delivery.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key + URL signature for access control. Secure URL signing prevents unauthorized transformations. imgix maintained. No formal compliance certifications listed. API key and signing key via environment variables. Signed URLs protect against URL manipulation attacks. Source images stored on customer-provided origin — imgix does not host originals.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: CDN SaaS with SLA and global status. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Purge operations required when source images change. CDN caching makes repeated transformation requests fast. Rate limits by plan — monitor for high-volume operations. URL-based transformation parameters are deterministic — same URL always produces same result.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Infura Ethereum and IPFS node infrastructure — JSON-RPC endpoint for agents to interact with blockchain without running a node. Simple API key (project ID). Supports Ethereum mainnet and testnets (Sepolia, Holesky). IPFS pinning via separate API. Rate limits documented by plan. WebSocket subscription for real-time blockchain events. Ethereum JSON-RPC methods are the primary interface — requires blockchain knowledge. Good for blockchain data reading agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS and WSS enforced. API key (project ID) auth — no scope granularity. Infura (ConsenSys) maintained. No formal compliance certifications listed. Project ID via environment variable. Read-only node queries (eth_call, eth_getBalance) are safe — signing and broadcasting transactions requires private key handling outside Infura. Never send private keys to Infura. Rate limits can cause silent JSON-RPC failures.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Managed blockchain node infrastructure with SLA and status page. Stable JSON-RPC API (Ethereum standard). Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Ethereum node sync state affects query freshness. WebSocket subscriptions require reconnection handling. Rate limits on free tier can cause dropped events. IPFS availability separate from Ethereum endpoint. Transaction submission idempotent (same signed tx always produces same result).

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server enabling agents to pause execution and request user input (quality 62). No auth required. No rate limits. Useful for human-in-the-loop agent workflows where confirmation is required before critical actions. Blocks agent execution until user responds — introduces latency. Limited documentation. Error handling if user doesn't respond is unclear. Good pattern for high-stakes agent actions requiring human approval.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local operation — no network exposure. No credentials required. User interaction is the access control mechanism. Unknown dependency hygiene. No compliance certifications. Human-in-the-loop pattern enhances security for high-stakes operations. Risk of UI spoofing if running in untrusted environment. Suitable for development and trusted local deployments.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted local tool with no SLA. Unknown version. No documented breaking change policy. Blocking execution model creates timeout risk for long-awaited user responses. No automatic timeout or fallback behavior documented. Good for development; production use requires careful timeout handling. User unavailability (AFK) blocks agent indefinitely without timeout.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Intercom customer messaging platform — conversations, users, companies, articles, outbound messaging. API key (token) or OAuth2. Rate limits documented (83/10 seconds on most endpoints). Conversation creation is not idempotent — retrying creates duplicates. User/contact deduplication using external_id. Webhooks for conversation and user events. Good for customer support automation agents. Search requires specific query format.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API key or OAuth2 — API key simpler for agents. Limited scope granularity. Intercom maintained. GDPR compliance for customer data. API key via environment variable. Customer conversation data contains PII — appropriate data governance required. Webhook HMAC signature verification required for production security. No read-only API key option documented.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA and status page. Stable versioned API. Good migration guides. Retry guidance documented. Rate limit 83/10sec — serialize bulk operations. Conversation creation not idempotent — use external_id for deduplication. Webhook delivery reliable with retry. Search API has separate rate limits. External ID pattern enables idempotent user upserts. Good overall reliability posture.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. IP geolocation API — country, region, city, timezone, currency, language from IP address. Simple API key (paid) or no key (limited free). Free tier limited to 1000/day without HTTPS. Rate limits documented. Batch lookup available. Good for geo-targeting and fraud detection agent workflows. Data accuracy varies by IP type (VPN, proxy, mobile). No write operations — read-only geolocation data.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS only on paid tier — free tier uses HTTP. API key auth — no scope granularity. Unknown dependency hygiene. No formal compliance certifications listed. API key via query parameter. IP address data may be considered PII in some jurisdictions — GDPR compliance required for EU users. VPN/proxy detection available but accuracy varies. HTTP exposure on free tier is a security concern.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with status page. Stable API. Good backward compatibility. Full GET idempotency on geolocation queries. Free tier limited to 1000/day — insufficient for production. HTTPS requirement on paid tier creates tier-dependent security. Batch lookups reduce request count for high-volume agents. IP geolocation data updated periodically — stale results possible for recently reassigned IPs.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. IPinfo IP intelligence — geolocation, ASN, hosted domains, privacy detection (VPN/proxy/Tor), abuse contacts. Simple API key. Free tier: 50,000 requests/month — generous for most agents. JSON response with comprehensive IP metadata. Batch lookups available. Excellent documentation. No write operations — read-only intelligence. Good for fraud detection, geo-targeting, and network analysis agent workflows.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Simple API key — no scope granularity. IPinfo maintained. GDPR compliance for IP data processing. API key via environment variable or Bearer token. IP addresses processed may be PII in some jurisdictions — appropriate legal basis required. Privacy detection data can identify VPN users — handle with appropriate data governance. Read-only intelligence platform minimizes risk.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SaaS with SLA. Stable API versioning. Good backward compatibility. Retry guidance documented. Full GET idempotency on all queries. Free tier 50k/month is generous for moderate agent workflows. Batch API reduces request count for high-volume operations. IP database updated regularly — data freshness is good. Downtime extremely rare based on track record.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server providing LLM access to iTerm2 terminal (macOS) (quality 50). CRITICAL: Gives LLM unrestricted shell access — highest blast radius of any tool category. No auth required beyond running locally. iTerm2 must be running on macOS. Minimal documentation. Error handling very limited. Not suitable for production agent workflows without strict sandboxing and human oversight. Acceptable only for supervised local development where human can intervene immediately.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local macOS operation only. No authentication or authorization for shell commands — full system access. No scope granularity — LLM can execute any command. Community-maintained with unknown security review. macOS local access controls provide minimal implicit boundary. CRITICAL RISK: Prompt injection attacks could execute arbitrary shell commands. No audit trail or command confirmation. Only use with explicit human oversight and never in automated pipelines.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — iTerm2 must be running. Unknown version — potentially unstable. No documented change policy. Shell commands may have irreversible effects — no rollback. Error handling for failed commands is limited. macOS only constrains deployment. iTerm2 version compatibility affects functionality. Not suitable for unattended or automated agent workflows.

af_score_components.notes: Local MCP server for JADX (Android APK/Java bytecode decompiler) (quality 65). No auth required — local tool. No rate limits. Enables LLM-assisted Android/Java reverse engineering: APK decompilation, class browsing, method analysis. JADX must be running with AI MCP plugin. Good for security research and Android malware analysis agent workflows. Decompiled output quality depends on obfuscation level. Useful niche tool for mobile security agents.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local JADX plugin — no network exposure. No credentials required or stored. Read-only decompilation operations. Open source JADX tool — dependency hygiene depends on JADX release. Analyzing malware APKs requires sandboxed environment — never analyze untrusted APKs on production systems. JADX license is open source (Apache 2.0). Local access controls provide implicit security boundary.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — JADX must be running with plugin. Version tied to JADX compatibility. No documented breaking change policy. Decompilation queries idempotent. Heavily obfuscated APKs may produce incomplete decompilation output. Large APKs consume significant memory. Plugin API stability tied to JADX release cadence. Good for specialized mobile security workflows.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Jenkins CI/CD REST API — trigger builds, check status, manage jobs. User API token for agents (avoid username/password). CSRF crumb token required for POST requests — extra step for agents. Build parameters via POST body. Build is async — must poll build number status. Rate limits depend on Jenkins server configuration — undocumented. Plugin ecosystem varies per instance — tool availability not guaranteed. Better MCP alternatives exist for CI/CD automation.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS only if configured — many Jenkins instances run HTTP internally. User API token (better than password) — no fine-grained permission scoping. CSRF crumb required for POST requests adds security. Community maintained — plugin dependency hygiene varies widely. API token via environment variable. Jenkins agent executors can run arbitrary code — significant blast radius if job triggers are exposed. Matrix security plugin enables RBAC but complex to configure.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted with no SLA. Stable REST API across recent Jenkins versions. Breaking changes occur between major versions. CSRF crumb expires — agents must refresh for each session. Build triggers async — must poll by build number. Rate limits from server configuration are undocumented. Plugin compatibility issues can cause unexpected failures. Build workspace management can cause disk pressure on long-running instances.

af_score_components.notes: MCP server for JetBrains AI integration (quality 72). OAuth auth required. Integrates AI capabilities into JetBrains IDE ecosystem. Rate limits from JetBrains AI service. JetBrains AI subscription required. MCP provides code completion, analysis, and generation in IDE context. Documentation moderate. Good for JetBrains-centric development agent workflows. Requires active JetBrains account and IDE.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 authentication via JetBrains account. JetBrains-maintained. Code submitted for analysis sent to JetBrains/AI provider servers — review data policy for sensitive codebases. OAuth tokens via secure flow. JetBrains AI subscription required — pricing adds to operational cost. No formal compliance certifications listed beyond standard JetBrains practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: JetBrains cloud infrastructure. Moderate version stability — tied to JetBrains AI product roadmap. Migration guides for major changes. OAuth token refresh required for long sessions. Rate limits on AI operations. JetBrains IDE required to be running. AI model updates may change behavior without version changes. Subscription expiry disrupts agent workflows.
